task: Research current public evidence for treating broad web research as a first-class AIOS capability route.
queries: Model Context Protocol official specification tools resources prompts 2026
OpenAI Agents SDK web search tool official documentation
Anthropic Model Context Protocol official docs tools resources prompts

sources.title: OpenAI API web search guide
claims: OpenAI documents web search as a tool that lets models access up-to-date internet information and return sourced citations.
The guide distinguishes quick search, agentic search, and deep research, which supports separate route choices by task depth.
The current Responses API path uses a web_search tool, while older preview tooling is treated as legacy for newer controls.

sources.title: OpenAI Agents SDK tools guide
claims: The Agents SDK exposes hosted tools including web search, file search, code interpreter, hosted MCP, image generation, and tool search.
The tools guide documents advanced web search controls such as filters, user location, and search context size.
The guide also describes tool search and namespaced deferred tools, supporting a capability-routing layer before execution.

sources.title: Model Context Protocol introduction
claims: MCP is presented as an open-source standard for connecting AI applications to external systems.
The introduction says MCP can connect AI applications to data sources, tools such as search engines, and workflows.
The docs state that MCP has broad ecosystem support across assistants and development tools.

sources.title: modelcontextprotocol/modelcontextprotocol repository
claims: The project repository contains the MCP specification, protocol schema, and official documentation.
The repository notes that the schema is TypeScript-first and also available as JSON Schema for broader compatibility.
The repository identifies modelcontextprotocol.io as the official documentation site.

synthesis_claims.claim: AIOS should model web research as a routeable capability because current agent platforms expose web search as an explicit tool rather than an implicit language-model behavior.
CapabilityOS should separate planning from execution: official tool ecosystems support tool routing and deferred tool surfaces, while execution should still return auditable evidence.
The ASC-0030 policy choice is justified: web route plans should require citations, freshness checks for current topics, privacy filtering, and bounded source opening.

limitations: This receipt records public documentation evidence only; it does not import facts into MemoryOS as accepted memory.
The actual web calls were performed by codex@myworld using the session web tool, not by CapabilityOS.
Future contracts should decide how MemoryOS reviews and retains selected web evidence.
